name: dual-collab-protocol-split
description: Socket.IO on /socket.io for SCENE_UPDATE, MAP_CAMERA_UPDATE, CURSOR, COMMENT (LWW). y-websocket on /yjs/:roomId for Yjs CRDT data-layer sync. NOT multiplexed — two separate TCP connections prevent Yjs catch-up (KB-MB) from blocking 60Hz cursor events via head-of-line blocking.

name: stub-then-wire
description: Security-sensitive or architecturally uncertain modules (e.g. yjs-crypto.ts) created as stubs in originating phase: API + tests, not wired into live path. Wiring deferred to next phase after explicit maintainer gate decision. Prevents coupling plan correctness to unresolved architectural questions.

Yjs E2EE naïve approach: encrypting inner payload before sending to setupWSConnection corrupts server Y.Doc. setupWSConnection calls Y.applyUpdate on received bytes directly — ciphertext corrupts the doc. Server-trusted relay and client-side E2EE are mutually exclusive with standard y-websocket.
resolution: unresolved — gate E-01 open. Options: (A) server-trusted no layer E2EE, (B) custom log-replay relay, (C) stub in Phase 5 evaluate in Phase 6. Recommended: Option C.

name: Yjs awareness wire format and Redis adapter namespacing
description: Awareness wire format: varUint(messageType) followed by varByteArray(payload) per y-protocols/awareness.js. Socket.IO Redis adapter key: 'atlasdraw:sio' (avoids collision with 'atlasdraw:yjs:*' y-websocket keys).
